text.title: Behavioral Design Patterns Worked Example
text.summary: One end-to-end worked example showing how behavioral-patterns produces a valid spec artefact.

example.title: Happy-path application
example.code:
--- artefact_id: behavioral-pattern-shipping-2026-05-23 owner: Ruslan Faion <ruslan@faion.net> ---
## Variance hotspot `shipping.calculate_cost` — 6-way if/elif on carrier ('dhl', 'ups', 'fedex', 'usps', 'royalmail', 'nova').
## Diagnosed pattern - Strategy — variance over algorithmic choice per carrier.
## Refactor sketch ```python class ShippingStrategy(Protocol): def cost(self, weight_kg: float, to: Country) -> Money: ...
class DhlStrategy: ... class UpsStrategy: ...
STRATEGIES: dict[str, ShippingStrategy] = { "dhl": DhlStrategy(), "ups": UpsStrategy(), ... }
def calculate_cost(carrier: str, weight: float, to: Country) -> Money: return STRATEGIES[carrier].cost(weight, to) ```
## Anti-misuse note Not State — the carrier is not a lifecycle stage. Not Chain of Responsibility — only one carrier handles the request.
example.note: Demonstrates the rules from 01-core-rules.xml applied to a concrete input; output validates against the schema in 02-output-contract.xml.
